source_jobs.title: Staff Software Engineer
source_jobs.description:
WHO WE ARE

School scheduling is a hard problem, one that technology was meant to solve. Yet for decades, the master schedule has been built by hand, consuming educators’ valuable time while delivering a sub-optimal result for students. We started Timely to build the tool we wish we had when we were in schools. And today, we are changing how schools create high quality schedules for teachers and students. Timely combines groundbreaking AI technology with support from a team of expert school schedulers: saving schedulers’ time, and surfacing opportunities for leaders to unlock staff potential. Timely delivers optimized master schedules that are more efficient, accommodate diverse educator needs, and enable student access to courses and pathways that impact their lives.

Our district and school partners agree: scheduling is a key opportunity for educational innovation, and that Timely is the indispensable partner in addressing the challenge. We’re experiencing exciting growth as a company, while doubling down on our mission and people-centered approach to leveraging powerful technology. We serve districts and charter school organizations across 15+ states, growing (and renewing) at rates that lead the education technology industry. This growth has been driven by our strong product-market fit, deep understanding of education, and commitment to customer success.


ABOUT THE ROLE

This role is for a senior engineer who is able to operate in ambiguous settings and straddle work in deeper engineering concerns alongside the product and business implications of their work. The engineer will be our founding team member for a new product initiative and as a result expected to lead both the product and technical discovery and execution early on in an independent manner. They will work closely with key stakeholders within the company as well as learning from existing and potential customers to define an MVP and future iterations.




KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Drive the early product discovery of a new area for Timely with some support from external PM, design, and business stakeholders. Coordinate and oversee work of these external stakeholders in the process.

 - Build initial prototypes and MVPs of the product end-end to prove value.

 - Establish & operationalize key decision processes and metrics to move forward with new product effort.

 - Drive key technical discovery and decisioning for any buildout. Rightsize our architecture and design early on to balance time to market and support for future iterations and integrations.

 - Engage with potential customers to engage with, provide feedback on, the product.




REQUIRED QUALIFICATIONS

 - Prior experience taking a product from 0 → 1 (either within a company or as a new startup)

 - Demonstrated flexibility in skillset to own engineering work alongside driving product and business decisions.

 - Strong technical ability: independently able to ship prototypes and features full stack.
   
   
   
   Includes competitive salary, comprehensive benefits, and meaningful equity

source_jobs.title: Senior Software Engineer, Platform
source_jobs.description:
WHO WE ARE

School scheduling is a hard problem, one that technology was meant to solve. Yet for decades, the master schedule has been built by hand, consuming educators’ valuable time while delivering a sub-optimal result for students. We started Timely to build the tool we wish we had when we were in schools. And today, we are changing how schools create high quality schedules for teachers and students. Timely combines groundbreaking AI technology with support from a team of expert school schedulers: saving schedulers’ time, and surfacing opportunities for leaders to unlock staff potential. Timely delivers optimized master schedules that are more efficient, accommodate diverse educator needs, and enable student access to courses and pathways that impact their lives.

Our district and school partners agree: scheduling is a key opportunity for educational innovation, and that Timely is the indispensable partner in addressing the challenge. We’re experiencing exciting growth as a company, while doubling down on our mission and people-centered approach to leveraging powerful technology. We serve districts and charter school organizations across 15+ states, growing (and renewing) at rates that lead the education technology industry. This growth has been driven by our strong product-market fit, deep understanding of education, and commitment to customer success.


ABOUT THE ROLE

This role is a Senior Software Engineer position with a strong focus on platform and infrastructure development. You and your team will own outcomes around stability, quality, maintainability, and speed of development for the Timely technical platform, encompassing infrastructure, tooling, development patterns, and critical shared libraries or components. In addition to identifying and delivering on projects that have the highest benefit for engineering team productivity and quality, you will help to up-level and onboard the entire team to best practices and establish a strong technology culture at Timely. 




KEY RESPONSIBILITIES: 

 - Identify, prioritize, design and deliver technical platform solutions spanning areas like cloud infrastructure, test infrastructure & patterns, shared product libraries, security practices, authentication platform and CI/CD tooling. 

 - Engage with engineering stakeholders to understand pain points and opportunities to enable Timely to deliver value quickly while maintaining quality. 

 - Drive the investigation and decisioning on key technology decisions impacting how Timely builds and maintains software. 

 - Own production availability by implementing observability and recovery practices for Timely applications while providing periodic on-call support. 

 - Uplevel the Timely engineering team on platform technologies and engineering patterns by maintaining documentation, presenting in different venues, providing cross-team design/code reviews, and mentoring team members. 

 - Own the delivery of critical projects by defining scope, creating tickets, and managing delivery timelines. 

 - Mentor and support junior engineers, promoting a culture of collaboration, knowledge-sharing, and continuous improvement.




REQUIRED QUALIFICATIONS

 - 3-5+ years of experience in infrastructure and platform engineering and comfort with the following (or equivalent) technologies: AWS, Terraform, MongoDB, Python, CI/CD via Github Actions. 

 - Ability to design and develop solutions full-stack to deliver value for the engineering team and wider business, including in the Frontend when necessary. 

 - Strong communication and collaboration skills, especially in cross-functional environments.

 - Commitment to continuous improvement: seeking out and welcoming code reviews, pairing, and collaborative problem-solving.

 - Experience working in agile environments and familiarity with testing frameworks (e.g., Jest, Pytest).

 - Experience and comfort operating in a fast startup environment and all that entails: strong ownership, self direc

source_jobs.title: Senior Software Engineer, Product
source_jobs.description:
WHO WE ARE

School scheduling is a hard problem, one that technology was meant to solve. Yet for decades, the master schedule has been built by hand, consuming educators’ valuable time while delivering a sub-optimal result for students. We started Timely to build the tool we wish we had when we were in schools. And today, we are changing how schools create high quality schedules for teachers and students. Timely combines groundbreaking AI technology with support from a team of expert school schedulers: saving schedulers’ time, and surfacing opportunities for leaders to unlock staff potential. Timely delivers optimized master schedules that are more efficient, accommodate diverse educator needs, and enable student access to courses and pathways that impact their lives.

Our district and school partners agree: scheduling is a key opportunity for educational innovation, and that Timely is the indispensable partner in addressing the challenge. We’re experiencing exciting growth as a company, while doubling down on our mission and people-centered approach to leveraging powerful technology. We serve districts and charter school organizations across 15+ states, growing (and renewing) at rates that lead the education technology industry. This growth has been driven by our strong product-market fit, deep understanding of education, and commitment to customer success.



About the Role

This role is for a Senior Fullstack Engineer who is able to operate in ambiguous settings and identify and deliver on projects in a fast-paced environment. As part of a founding team for a new initiative at Timely, you will work closely and cross-functionally with product and success teams for technical discovery, prototyping, and execution. In this role, you will also work with key stakeholders in the company and learn from existing and potential customers as you create an MVP and future iterations that solve customer problems and are aligned to our business objectives.


Responsibilities

 - Support discovery in a new area, in collaboration with Product, Success, and other business stakeholders.

 - Collaborate with Product to rapid build initial prototypes and MVPs, owning the engineering workflow to identify, scope, prioritize, and deliver technical solutions.

 - Drive technical decisioning and buildout, rightsizing our architecture early to on to balance time to market and support for future iterations and integrations.

 - Facilitate quick iteration and learning by applying customer feedback to initial delivered prototypes.

 - Communicate progress and findings, engage with internal and external stakeholders to gather and implement feedback
   
   

About You

 - Mindset: You are a self-starter who is comfortable working in ambiguity and passionate about creating opportunities in education. You are excited by complex problems and imaginative solutions. You take on new challenges eagerly and are driven to see ideas through to completion. You take ownership, communicate progress effectively, and adapt quickly in a fast-paced startup environment.

 - Experience: At least 3 years designing and implementing progressively more complex full-stack engineering features and products (our stack is React, Python, AWS, MongoDB, but you don’t have to be limited to these specific technologies).

 - Communication and collaboration skills, especially in engaging non-technical users or stakeholders.

 - Comfort operating in a fast startup environment and leveraging AI

 - Nice-to-have (not required):
   
   - Background in EdTech and/or education sector
   
   - Experience taking a product from 0 to 1 either within a company or as a new startup
     
     

Why Join Us

 - Work with an exceptional team tackling meaningful challenges in education.

 - Be part of a growing, financially healthy company with a proven product.

 - Enjoy comprehensive benefits, including health insurance, parental leave, 401(k), unlimited PTO, access to coworking, and equity in a fast-gr

source_jobs.title: Product Manager
source_jobs.description:
WHO WE ARE

School scheduling is a hard problem, one that technology was meant to solve. Yet for decades, the master schedule has been built by hand, consuming educators’ valuable time while delivering a sub-optimal result for students. We started Timely to build the tool we wish we had when we were in schools. And today, we are changing how schools create high quality schedules for teachers and students. Timely combines groundbreaking AI technology with support from a team of expert school schedulers: saving schedulers’ time, and surfacing opportunities for leaders to unlock staff potential. Timely delivers optimized master schedules that are more efficient, accommodate diverse educator needs, and enable student access to courses and pathways that impact their lives.

Our district and school partners agree: scheduling is a key opportunity for educational innovation, and that Timely is the indispensable partner in addressing the challenge. We’re experiencing exciting growth as a company, while doubling down on our mission and people-centered approach to leveraging powerful technology. We serve districts and charter school organizations across 15+ states, growing (and renewing) at rates that lead the education technology industry. This growth has been driven by our strong product-market fit, deep understanding of education, and commitment to customer success.



About our Product

At Timely, our product does the impossible: it makes the job of a school scheduler measurably easier, while also delivering better quality in the results. We deliver optimized master schedules that are more efficient, accommodate diverse educator needs, and enable student access to courses and pathways that impact their lives. We address the needs of school-based employees who toil away building schedules and central office employees who need visibility into them.



We differentiate our product in the market through a combination of groundbreaking optimization technology plus an intuitive workflow experience, guiding users through options, implications, and solutions – supported by an experienced Success team.



About the Role

This role is for an experienced product manager who is able to operate in ambiguous settings and consider both the design and business implications of their work. As part of a founding team for a new initiative at Timely, you will work closely and cross-functionally with Engineering and Success teams to uncover customer challenges, develop and test solutions, and drive impactful product decisions in new areas.


This role will balance customer needs with business strategy - identifying the right problems to solve, deciding which solutions to build, and making sure the entire organization understands not just what we’re building, but why. You’ll ensure product decisions are clear, priorities are well-communicated, and our roadmap reflects both user impact and company goals.



Responsibilities

 - Lead early product discovery in a new area to identify and prioritize customer problems, in collaboration with Engineering stakeholders for technical discovery.

 - Lead ideation and design exercises to generate potential solutions and approaches to solving customer problems, leveraging the Success team and other stakeholders.

 - Write product stories that include the “why,” “what,” and “when,” while working closely with Engineering to define the “how”.

 - Collaborate with Engineering stakeholders to build initial prototypes and MVPs.

 - Establish goal- and metric-setting processes with Engineering, to motivate and measure the work.

 - Engage with potential customers to ideate and gather feedback on product and prototypes.

 - Foster a culture of delivering value by setting and adjusting milestones, maintaining high standards, and keeping the team focused on impact.

 - Communicate product decisions with clarity and enthusiasm, ensuring internal and external stakeholders understand priorities, and the reasoning behind them.

notes: AI-powered school scheduling tool with strong engineering focus
